response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned generalist with strong web and full-stack credentials, but he misses nearly every core requirement of this role. There is no indication of native-level Japanese proficiency (he lists English, Romanian, and basic German), professional C-for-UNIX experience is limited to a brief six-month stint at National Instruments a decade ago, and there is no evidence of Linux kernel source modification, embedded LAN-switch firmware, or security technologies such as MACsec or 802.1X. His career trajectory points firmly toward TypeScript/Node.js product engineering, not embedded network security.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded contexts
Low-latency systems experience at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic (async I/O, fibers, high-throughput services)
Strong engineering-craft practices: TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions, and technical teaching
concerns: No native-level Japanese proficiency, which is a hard requirement for the role
Minimal professional C/UNIX experience and no demonstrated Linux kernel analysis or modification work
No background in L2/L3 networking, switch products, or security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal)

key_factors.factor: Lack of C/UNIX and Linux kernel experience
General seniority and systems programming exposure
